description_long: This is a large, single-story log cabin with a sprawling, multi-wing layout set on a rectangular grassy plot. The structure features a dark, multi-gabled roof with deep overhangs and exposed timber beams, giving it a robust and rustic appearance. The exterior walls are constructed from thick, natural wood logs, and the cabin is surrounded by a paved stone walkway that outlines its perimeter. Multiple large windows with wooden frames are symmetrically distributed across the visible facades, allowing ample natural light into the interior. Several covered porch areas are integrated into the design, providing sheltered outdoor spaces at the front and sides. The overall style is reminiscent of a craftsman or lodge-inspired log cabin, blending traditional craftsmanship with modern comfort.
description: A sprawling, single-story log cabin with a dark, multi-gabled roof, exposed timber beams, and large windows, set on a grassy plot with stone walkways. Covered porches and a rustic, craftsman-inspired design define its appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a prominent covered porch, symmetrical gabled wings, and large windows flanking the central entryway.
description_view_1: The left-side view highlights the depth of the structure, the extended rooflines, and additional covered porch areas along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals more large windows and a secondary entrance, with the stone walkway wrapping around the back and side wings.
description_view_3: The right-side view displays the cabin's multi-wing layout, additional porches, and the continuity of the stone walkway around the building.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gabled roof with deep overhangs and exposed timber beams, constructed from dark roofing material
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple large rectangular windows symmetrically distributed across all visible facades, often grouped in pairs or threes, with wooden frames and clear glass
attribute_door_placement: Main entrance door centered on the front facade beneath the largest gable, with additional doors visible on the rear and side wings, each integrated into covered porch areas
